sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the image, the center cell is now green (not white). So clicking on the center cell toggled it from gray to green. The grid now has:
- White cells: top, left, right, bottom
- Green cell: center
- Gray cells: corners
This is not the pattern I want. I want all cross cells to be white (or all the same color).
Let me think about this more carefully. The grid cells seem to cycle through colors: gray → green → white → gray → ...
When I click on a cell, it changes to the next color in the cycle.
So to make all cross cells white, I need to click on the green center cell again to change it from green to white.
Wait, but earlier when I clicked on cells, they changed between gray and green. Let me reconsider the color cycle.
Actually, looking at the history:
1. Initial state: all cells gray
2. After clicking on center cell with cursor at right end: center cell becomes green
3. After clicking on other cells: they become green
4. After moving cursor to right end and triggering game over: cross cells become white
5. After clicking on center cell: center cell becomes gray (from white)
6. After clicking on center cell again: center cell becomes green (from gray)
So the color cycle seems to be: gray → green → white → gray → ...
To make all cross cells white, I need to click on the green center cell to change it from green to white.
Let me try clicking on the center cell again.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# The center cell is now green. I need to click on it again to change it to white.
# Let me click on the center cell to toggle it from green to white.
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 55, 'col': 30}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
